title: Eva 02 Blood Color Change
wikitext:
[]: Eva 02 Blood Color Change             Quote Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 06, 2010 12:11 pm Ok, i tried using the search for starters but even tough i did find some old debates about the Unit-02 Blood being blue, none of those seemed conclusive, in either way, i tought it would be interesting for us to debate: 1) Why was the Eva-02 blood the only one Blue to start with in the NGE, even tough most angels, and the others Adams clone have Red blood? 2) Is there a phisical reason for the Nerv detecting Angels and adressing them as Blue-Blood? and the main reason: 3) Why did they change the Unit-02 Blue blood to Red in the NME? Even tough i never made much sense out of it, i always tought Unit 02 was even cooler thatn Unit 01 by having blue blood, being red and having four eyes =X Imgs: [URL=http://img683.imageshack.us/i/grab18109.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://img153.imageshack.us/i/grab131107.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Yossarian » Sun Jun 06, 2010 12:22 pm 1) Hemocyanin instead of Hemoglobin? Which would produce a bluish blood. Or Gainax ran outa red when coloring. 2) Technobabble 3) To avoid confusion possibly? They finally had all the red they could ever need? I too always loved the 4-eyed Eva, with it's epic chin. Leno's got nothing on Unit-02!

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Sun Jun 06, 2010 1:06 pm 1. Damn over imaginative animaters. 2. I agree with Yorrarian, it's Technobabble 3. Better animaters.

[]:             Quote Post by IkariRei » Sun Jun 06, 2010 3:59 pm The first time i watched Rebuild 1.0 i noticed Sachiel's blood is changed from blue to red. I guess they're really inconsistent. Shouldn't they just made it all blue considering the analysis pattern is blue and evas are made from 1st and 2nd angels.. Maybe they wanted to put more gore in the anime and it doesn't really feel that goreish if the blood isn't red..

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 06, 2010 4:02 pm The only reason Unit 02's blood wasn't red in the first place was probably due to fear of censorship. It's pretty crazy that Unit 01 bleeds so much red as it is, but Unit 02 gets mutilated way worse. It's also possible that Unit 01's blood was red whereas the blood of the other Evas was blue as a result of Unit 01's birth from Lilith instead of Adam, but since After Sachiel the Angels' blood color was never kept consistent anyway, that wouldn't have worked out. And in the new movies the Angel blood is red, so it wouldn't have meant anything anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by IkariRei » Sun Jun 06, 2010 4:15 pm ^Was thinking the same but Eva 00's blood is also red so it looks totally random...or they were drunk..

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sun Jun 06, 2010 5:13 pm I just think they didn't want it to bleed red because the eva itself was already red. It wasn't very clever and logical anyway, so it's a good thing that they stick with the red blood now.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sun Jun 06, 2010 5:31 pm Eva-02's blood was probably colored blue simply because they wanted it to contrast with her red armor. In Rebuild they've really played up red blood as a visual motif, so they might have wanted to stay more consistent.

[]:             Quote Post by Number 44 » Sun Jun 06, 2010 5:36 pm I had sited blood color as inconsistency in another thread, and got a a bunch of disagreement... You sir, have made my day.

[]: Re: [Spoiler] Eva 02 Blood Color Change             Quote Post by Lightfrequency » Mon Jun 07, 2010 4:36 am sephirotic wrote: 2) Is there a phisical reason for the Nerv detecting Angels and adressing them as Blue-Blood

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Mon Jun 07, 2010 8:19 am Yossarian wrote: I too always loved the 4-eyed Eva, with it's epic chin.

[]: Re: [Spoiler] Eva 02 Blood Color Change             Quote Post by EdZ » Mon Jun 07, 2010 8:31 am Lightfrequency wrote: particles vibrate at certain frequencies which correspond to particular light wave frequencies, I always assumed angels vibrated at a frequency of blue

[]: Re: [Spoiler] Eva 02 Blood Color Change             Quote Post by Lightfrequency » Mon Jun 07, 2010 9:09 am EdZ wrote: Then the Angels would be glowing blue. The 'Pattern:Blue' technobabble is just to tell viewers "this is unambigously an angel".

[]:             Quote Post by Yossarian » Mon Jun 07, 2010 11:09 am Nice!

[]: Re: [Spoiler] Eva 02 Blood Color Change             Quote Post by EdZ » Mon Jun 07, 2010 1:54 pm Lightfrequency wrote: the particles vibrate at a frequency that corresponds to the wavelength frequency of blue, that doesn't mean they glow blue, all particles vibrate go read a physics book.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 07, 2010 2:03 pm But they're Particle-WAVES, and this is said as if it is somehow special and different from normal, everyday matter. Clearly, we need more research on the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Mon Jun 07, 2010 2:13 pm Legendary wrote: But they're Particle-WAVES, and this is said as if it is somehow special and different from normal, everyday matter. Clearly, we need more research on the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 07, 2010 2:14 pm Particle-wave matter of Evangelion is stated to be DIFFERENT from normal matter. They say it's "like light", but that's it.

[]:             Quote Post by Yossarian » Mon Jun 07, 2010 4:38 pm So yes, It's technobabble, plain and simple.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Mon Jun 07, 2010 7:45 pm 2) TOO MUCH RED ! i remember when i first saw the tv series, Asuka costume and the evas were so brigh red, you could barely make out the lines in them. having red blood flowing of a red main body wouldnt look as great as another colour,in those days they used paint to paint over a cel, and diferent paints are expensive. Remember batman tas ? the colours were so bright not to contrast with the dark backgrounds, but becouse they had to reuse the paints from FREAKING TINY TOONS, as new colours would be expensive to use, one of the reason robin dark green paint, was actually dark blue. Today with digital that limitation is gone. and the hues from blood and main body are distinct enough.
